perturbation.original_text: In connection with the consideration of a possible negotiated transaction (a “ Possible Transaction ”) between the Parties and/or their respective subsidiaries (each such Party being hereinafter referred to, collectively with its subsidiaries, as a “ Company ”), each Company (in its capacity as a provider of information hereunder, a “ Provider ”) is prepared to make available to the other Company (in its capacity as a recipient of information hereunder, a “ Recipient ”) certain “Evaluation Material” (as defined in Section 2 below) in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and to take or refrain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with the consideration of a possible negotiated transaction (a “ Potential Venture ”) between the Parties and/or their respective subsidiaries (each such Party being hereinafter referred to, collectively with its subsidiaries, as a “ Company ”), each Company (in its capacity as a provider of information hereunder, a “ Provider ”) is prepared to make available to the other Company (in its capacity as a recipient of information hereunder, a “ Recipient ”) certain “Evaluation Material” (as defined in Section 2 below) in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and to take or refrain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Possible Transaction” to “Potential Venture” creates an ambiguity. The agreement heavily references 'Possible Transaction'. By calling it 'Potential Venture', it introduces uncertainty if the subsequent use of 'Possible Transaction' still applies or whether both are same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Definitions . (a) The term “Evaluation Material” means information concerning the Provider which has been or is furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent they contain or are based upon the information furnished by the Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information. (b) The term “Representatives” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable. (c) The term “Person” includes the media and any corporation, partnership, group, individual or other entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the transaction as a 'Possible Transaction'. The changed text replaces this with 'Potential Venture'. The contradicted text in section 2(a) defines 'Evaluation Material' in the context of the Recipient’s evaluation of a 'Possible Transaction'. By changing 'Possible Transaction' to 'Potential Venture', the agreement introduces uncertainty as to whether the definition of 'Evaluation Material' still applies to the 'Potential Venture'. This creates a contradiction because it's no longer clear if section 2(a) is related to the modified text.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s 1 Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Receiver shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Receiver’s 1 Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Receiver evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'Receiver' creates a confusion. The document defines 'Recipient' in detail. If 'Receiver' is not defined, it is unclear whether the obligations and rights are same as for the 'Recipient', particularly regarding responsibility for Representative conduct, mentioned further down.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Recipient' to 'Receiver' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly states that 'Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives.' The change to 'Receiver' introduces ambiguity, since Receiver is not defined and it is unclear whether the same responsibility applies. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each User agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Recipient” with “User” creates an immediate contradiction, since Recipient is referred to in the next sentense. This is especially problematic since Representatives are defined only in context with the “Recipient”, not the “User”, it creates confusion about who is accountable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Recipient" to "User" directly contradicts the contradicted text because the contradicted text uses the term "Recipient" and "Representatives", and Representatives are defined only in context with "Recipient". The change creates a strong inconsistency, rendering the second sentence unclear and potentially unenforceable, because the "Representatives" are defined only in relation to 'Recipient', creating a direct conflict within the document. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: If a Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 above, such Recipient shall provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement together with copies of the material proposed to be disclosed so that the Provider may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: If a Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Information or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 above, such Recipient shall provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement together with copies of the material proposed to be disclosed so that the Provider may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation Material" to "Information" in Section 5 creates ambiguity because "Evaluation Material" is a defined term in Section 2, while "Information" is not. The section then refers to 'facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 above', which pertains to Evaluation Material. This creates confusion about what must be disclosed, and what is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, a Recipient or its Representatives are nonetheless legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 or otherwise be liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, such Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally required to disclose, provided that the Recipient and/or its Representatives cooperate with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Evaluation Material" to "Information" creates a contradiction because "Evaluation Material" is a defined term in Section 2 and Section 4, as described in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text explicitly refers to "Evaluation Material" and facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4, but the Changed Text expands the scope to "Information", which isn't defined, creating ambiguity. This change directly conflicts with the Contradicted Text because it is no longer clear whether the Recipient is legally compelled to disclose 'Information' or only 'Evaluation Material,' leading to an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the scope of the disclosure obligation.

perturbation.original_text: If either Company decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Company of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to, within five business days of receipt of such notice, destroy or return all Evaluation Material, except for that portion of notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which does not contain in any respect any of the Evaluation Materials, in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained, except that Recipient’s outside legal counsel may retain one copy to be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of establishing Recipient’s compliance with its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: If either Company decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Company of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Supplier for any reason, a Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to, within five business days of receipt of such notice, destroy or return all Evaluation Material, except for that portion of notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which does not contain in any respect any of the Evaluation Materials, in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained, except that Recipient’s outside legal counsel may retain one copy to be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of establishing Recipient’s compliance with its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Provider" to "Supplier" in Section 6 leads to an internal contradiction. The paragraph describes actions triggered by the Provider's request. If it's now the 'Supplier', the obligations related to the original definition of 'Provider' become uncertain, particularly concerning return of evaluation material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence signed by an executive officer of the Recipient. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by such Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material. 2
perturbation.justification: The change from "Provider" to "Supplier" creates a contradiction. The original text explicitly outlines obligations and actions triggered by the 'Provider' and its requests, specifically regarding the return of Evaluation Material and the provision of a compliance certificate. The 'Contradicted Text' also refers to obligations to the 'Provider'. By changing 'Provider' to 'Supplier', the text introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding who can trigger these obligations related to the Evaluation Material, directly conflicting with the 'Contradicted Text' that assumes the continued existence and relevance of the term 'Provider'.
